title: Jacobaea subalpina
taxonomy: Jacobaea subalpina is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is sometimes classified as Senecio subalpinus, as it was previously placed in the genus Senecio. However, it is currently recognized as part of the Jacobaea genus. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification for this species.
uses: Jacobaea subalpina is known to have some traditional medicinal uses, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied. In traditional medicine, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots and leaves of Jacobaea subalpina are often used to make teas, infusions, and other preparations. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Jacobaea subalpina as a medicinal plant. It is also essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.

properties: Jacobaea subalpina, also known as Alpine hawk's beard, has been traditionally used in medicine for its therapeutic properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and heal wounds. The plant's leaves and stems contain sesquiterpene lactones, compounds that have been shown to have potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Jacobaea subalpina's therapeutic properties may also include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using the plant for medicinal purposes.

intro: Jacobaea subalpina, also known as the Alpine Ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, with its leaves and flowers exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antitussive properties, which can help alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Jacobaea subalpina include sesquiterpene lactones, particularly jacozine and subalpinine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Jacobaea subalpina is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried preparations for respiratory issues and coughs.
